title: Compare decimals through thousandths
description: Compare 2 decimals with digits up to the thousandths using >, <, and = symbols.
topic_path: Module 1: Place value and decimal fractions
Topic B: Decimal fractions and place value patterns

hints:
Let's use a place value table to compare our two numbers.

We can line up the decimal points and compare starting with the greatest place value.  

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\blueE{3}$| $7$ |$.$  | $5$ | $9$ | $8$
$\blueE0$| $3$ |$.$  | $7$ | $5$ | $8$

The greatest place value with different digits is the tens place.

$37.508$ has $\blueE3$ tens.  $3.758$ has $\blueE0$ tens.

Since $\blueE37.508$ has more tens, it is greater than $\blueE03.758$.

Let's use a place value table to compare our two numbers.

We can line up the decimal points and compare starting with the greatest place value.  

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$9$ |$.$  | $3$ | $\blueD7$ | $0$
$9$ |$.$  | $3$ | $\blueD0$ | $7$

The greatest place value with different digits is the hundredths place.

$9.3\blueD70$ has $\blueD7$ hundredths. $9.3\blueD07$ has $\blueD0$ hundredths. 

Since $9.3\blueD70$  has more hundredths, it is greater than $9.3\blueD07$.

$1$| $6$ |$.$ | $\blueD5$ | $0$
$1$| $6$ |$.$  | $\blueD4$ | $5$
The greatest place value with different digits is the tenths place.

$16.\blueD50$ has $\blueD5$ tenths.  $16.\blueD45$ has $\blueD4$ tenths.

Since $16.\blueD50$  has more tenths, it is greater than $16.\blueD45$.

The greatest place value with different digits is the tenths place.

The greatest place value with different digits is the tenths place.

$1.\blueD011$ has $\blueD0$ tenths. $1.\blueD101$ has $\blueD1$ tenth. 

Since $1.\blueD011$  has fewer tenths, it is less than $1.\blueD101$.
